ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Daniel Cunningham by zoom.
Melanie Emmons appeared by zoom for Gary Wayne Hennigh (Beneficiary).

The Court raised the issue of payment of fees on account, stated that it understands that mistakes get made, that it hopes it allowed lessons to be learned, asked what steps are being taken to stop this and asked about the over payment to Mr. Valenti. Mr. Cunningham stated that he has taken significant changes in his practice to make sure payment in error doesn't happen again, that he made a change in the software and in the office staff, and that Mr. Valenti is going to mail a check for $912.00 to him. Ms. Alavi addressed the Attorney's fees stating that she made sure they are not duplicate fees, that it was a matter of all of the employees having not run their time for services that were a benefit to the trust and that the invoices are usually presented at the time of the accounting. Ms. Alavi also addressed the issue of the investments stating that they were informed that those investments (three vanguard funds) no longer exist and that the request is no longer needed. Ms. Emmons stated that the Beneficiary had been in and out of the hospital, that the Beneficiary would like a 30 to 45-day continuance for her to go over the accounting with him in person, that historically he has felt that he hasn't been heard and that it is important to him. In addition, Ms. Emmons stated that she heard from her Client two days ago, and that they have spoken over the phone, but she was unable to recently because he was in and out of the hospital. The Court noted that this has already been continued twice in two months after the initial hearing. Ms. Alavi stated that she concurs with the Court, that the accounting was mailed to Mr. Hennigh and his Attorney, that Mr. Cunningham has spoken with Mr. Hennigh and that she leaves it up to the Court. Mr. Cunningham stated that Mr. Hennigh is requesting the same things repeatedly which are duplicates of the accounting, and that he provided the bank statements and the accounting in a blown-up form so that Mr. Hennigh could see the documents. The Court stated that there has been plenty of notice and opportunity to object, that it has receieved no objections and that it is time to move this forward. The Amended Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. is under submission.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons for and with Gary Hennigh (Primary Beneficiary).
Liza De Vries for and with Debra Dolch (Petitioner).
Avid Alavi for and with Daniel Cunningham (Proposed Successor Trustee).
Charles Jonas Guardian Ad Litem for Gary Hennigh.

1) No one responded to the Court's inquiry as to whether anyone is objecting to the Petition for Order Accepting Resignation of Debra Dolch as Trustee. The Court granted said petition (subject to review of the order).

3) Ms. Alavi stated that she looked over the Trust that was prepared by Ms. Dolch, that she and Ms. Emmons came to agreement on several provisions except for three terms (suspension of allowance during incarceration, hospitalization for substance abuse and rehabilitation/ protection against dissipation of the Trust /termination of Trust at age 60), and that she is deferring to Ms. De Vries to address the issue of termination of the Trust. Ms. Emmons and Ms. De Vries presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to what the Trustors intentions were, the provision with respect to Mr. Hennigh receiving principal at age 60, whether there is a contingency of behavior, the Trustor amending the Trust in 2005 to leave Mr. Hennigh as Successor Trustee, made reference to provisions and boiler plate language in the Trust instrument as well as the intake form, etc. All Counsel indicated that they are okay with Mr. Cunningham providing services as a Case Manager. The Petition for Order Instructing Trustee Whether to Terminate Trust and Make Final Distribution of Trust Assets, etc. is taken under submission. Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Successor Trustee To Pay From Trust Assets (1) Criminal-Defense Attorneys Fees Relating To Two New Felony Cases Currently Pending Against Primary Trust Beneficiary, Gary Wayne Hennigh; And (2) Trust Attorneys Fees For Gary Wayne Hennigh
Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lizabeth DeVries for and with Debra Dolch. Charles Jonas, Guardian ad Litem for Mr. Hennigh.

The Court expressed its concerns regarding interpreting the requirements/limitations of the trust, payments to criminal attorney's and whether or not it should be permitted if the fees are associated with getting him into a program. Ms. DeVries reported that the criminal court approved a plea this morning, that he will be required to attend a treatment program, submit to testing, will no longer be able to drive, 5 years probation, that it resolves all of the cases, that the DUI charges include prescription medicines which he will be on for the rest of his life, that he has been cut off of SSI & disability due to his incarceration, that Ms. Canny has worked to make the DUI's not be felonies, has had to meet with him daily and is an integral part of getting him treatment. The Court stated that it understands this has been a difficult job for Ms. Canny but that it is different than paying the doctor who runs the clinic. Ms. DeVries stated that the underlying documents of the trust show that Ms. Hennigh wanted Gary dissuaded from his lifestyle, that the trustor was to do whatever was necessary and that the trust language does not limit payment to doctors, just gives it as an example. The Court reminded counsel that earlier probations required various programs which Gary did not participate in. Ms. DeVries further stated that Mr. Boessenecker is the first attorney to step up and agree to work with Gary, that he is an advocate regarding benefits that would provide care, that a cap on his fees can be put in place, that Ms. Canny's bill is expected to be $50,000.00 as she has put in an exorbitant amount of time with Gary and that the car accident made him dependent on prescription drugs, something his mother would not have known at the time of writing the trust. Mr. Jonas reported that Gary calls on a regular basis, that he has no idea if Gary will succeed in the program, that Ms. Canny's role has been to act as a conduit to get Gary to agree to a program, that Gary has not been diagnosed as an alcoholic, that the trust ambiguities make it hard to work with Gary and that the cost of the criminal attorney is within a reasonable interpretation of the trust. Ms. Dolch reported that the major asset in the trust is property which is vacant and that the trust is worth $1,200,000. Mr. Jonas stated that Gary needs a trust attorney to represent him, that Mr. Boessenecker will do a fine job and that if the trust will not pay the fees he will not have an attorney. The Court asked if there is a general provision in the trust allowing the trustee to pay out for the general well being of the beneficiary and what the court's role is in making a determination regarding payments. Ms. DeVries referred to an 11/24/14 order giving the trustee broader discretionary authority and that the court's role is to look at the bills and approve if valid. The Court asked if authority to pay Ms. Canny could be conditioned on Gary entering treatment. Mr. Jonas stated that Ms. Canny has secured the agreement and that if Gary fails he goes to prison. Ms. Dolch suggested that a marker could be that he enters a treatment program. Ms. DeVries stated that Ms. Canny has been operating in good faith, that Gary is not mentally capable of getting into a program without help and that he has no other funds to pay an attorney. The Court stated that the proposed order regarding Ms. Canny's fees is payment not to exceed $25,000 prior to trial and $25,000 if the proceedings fail and that there is no estimate from Mr. Boessenecker. Ms. DeVries stated that that was the estimate when this was filed in July, that they could not predict what would happen and that Mr. Boessenecker did not think his services would exceed $10,000. The petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order (1) Determining Questions Of Construction Of Trust Instrument; (2) Determining Powers And/Or Duties Of Trustee; And, (3) Granting Powers To Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Liza De Vries for and with Debra Dolch. Charles Jonas Guardian Ad Litem for Gary Hennigh.

Mr. Jonas reported that he spoke with Mr. Hennigh about the proposals to resolve the ambiguities, that Mr. Hennigh indicated a desire to be represented and that he is amenable to going to a treatment center, and that Mr. Hennigh would like to succeed Ms. Dolch and petition to have himself appointed. Ms. De Vries stated spoke of the criminal matter, stated that Mr. Hennigh has not revealed the amount of his disability checks, that she chose a December 9th hearing date on the Petition for Order Clarifying/Modifying Certain Terms of The Trust prior to being aware of the fourth incident and in hopes that Mr. Hennigh would clean up his act and be in court in person. Discussion was held regarding Mr. Hennigh's condition, whether or not a conservatorship would be helpful, whether it is appropriate to keep up the Pacifica property, the issue of the Trustee having discretion to invade principal and defining for what purpose, deferring the drug testing, whether there are other benefits Mr. Hennigh may be entitled to, whether those funds could be used for Criminal Counsel, the issue of costs being driven by Mr. Hennigh, the Trustee having discretion to determine when the allowance can commence, Ms. Dolch looking into whether Mr. Hennigh's dental needs are being met, whether Mr. Hennigh owns any vehicles/motorcycles, etc. The Court stated that it does not see any reason to pay an allowance while Mr. Hennigh is incarcerated, no reason to pay the credit cards and that it does not think the citations should be paid at this time. The Petition for Order Determining Questions of Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. matter was taken under submission.

Petition For Appointment Of Debra J. Dolch As Sole Successor Trustee Of The Pearl A. Hennigh Trust D
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Liza De Vries for and with Debra Dolch. Paul Melbostad also present.

Ms. Dolch spoke of some of the assets she has marshaled and the multiple financial institutions she has dealt with, and stated that she assisted Gary Hennigh (Son) in receiving $27,000.00 in death benefits from US Steele, IRA benefits and an $85.00 pension. Ms. Dolch also spoke of Gary's circumstances and stated that Gary was supposed to be present today, that he demanded a 60-day continuance because he wants to hire Counsel and that bond should be at $1,000,000.00. Ms. De Vries stated that the Trust should be under Court supervision so that the Court can provide stability and oversight to secure everyone's interest, and raised the issue of appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem for Gary. The Court stated that it was inclined to not continue the matter, appoint Ms. Dolch as Successor Trustee with a bond of $1,000,000.00, approve amounts on account at $1,500.00 per month and bring the Trust under Court supervision. The Petition for Appointment of Debra Dolch as Sole Successor Trustee, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
